name: vultr-blade-mcp
title: Vultr GPU Cloud
description: Manage Vultr GPU infrastructure — create, start, stop, and delete cloud VMs with GPU provisioning and cloud-init deployment. 12 tools implementing the virtualmachine-v1 contract.
tagline: GPU cloud management — VM lifecycle, plans, regions, and cloud-init provisioning
author: groupthink-dev
contract_notes: Full virtualmachine-v1 implementation. All 5 required ops, 2 recommended, 5 gated. GPU-first: plans default to GPU type filter.

highlights.text: 12 tools — full VM lifecycle: create, start, stop, delete, reboot
GPU-first — plans default to GPU type with VRAM and pricing
Cloud-init — inject user_data for automated GPU worker deployment
Dual write-gate — env var + per-call confirm for all destructive ops

readme:
### What it does

Vultr GPU Cloud provides full control over Vultr VM instances through 12 MCP tools. Provision GPU-accelerated VMs, deploy with cloud-init, and manage the complete VM lifecycle — all from your AI agent.

### Tool coverage

| Domain | Tools | What you can do |
|--------|-------|-----------------|
| Instances | 3 | List, get details, check status |
| VM lifecycle | 5 | Create, delete, start, stop, reboot |
| Plans | 1 | List plans with GPU specs and pricing |
| Regions | 1 | List available datacentres |
| Images | 1 | List OS images |
| SSH Keys | 1 | List keys for injection |

### GPU provisioning flow

List GPU plans (`type=vcg`), pick a region, select an OS image, inject cloud-init user_data (installs Docker + NVIDIA toolkit + pulls your OCI image), and create. Poll status until `server_status=ok`. Delete when done.

### Token efficiency

Formatters strip ~60% of Vultr's verbose API responses. Plans prominently display GPU specs (type, VRAM, count) and pricing. Responses are capped at 4000 characters with pagination guidance.

### Safety

All write operations require dual confirmation: `VULTR_WRITE_ENABLED=true` environment variable AND `confirm: true` per-call parameter. Creating instances incurs GPU compute charges immediately.

env.name: VULTR_API_KEY
env.description: Vultr API key for authentication — all tools require this

env.name: VULTR_WRITE_ENABLED
env.description: Set to 'true' to enable write operations (create, delete, start, stop, reboot)

setup.blurb: GPU cloud management — 12 tools for VM lifecycle, GPU provisioning, and cloud-init deployment across Vultr's global datacentres.
setup.help_text: Create an API key at my.vultr.com/settings/#settingsapi. The key provides access to your Vultr account including creating instances (which incur compute charges).

fields.label: API Key
fields.help: Full-access API key. Instance creation incurs GPU compute charges immediately.

scenarios.label: Provision a GPU VM with cloud-init
scenarios.body: List GPU plans, pick the cheapest A100, select a region near you, create an instance with cloud-init user_data that installs Docker and pulls a GPU worker image, then poll status until ready.

scenarios.label: Cost audit — find idle instances
scenarios.body: List all instances, check their power status and cost, identify stopped instances still incurring charges, and delete any that are no longer needed.

scenarios.label: Compare GPU plan pricing
scenarios.body: List all GPU plans with pricing, sort by hourly cost, and identify the best value for A100, L40S, and A16 GPU types across regions.

notes: First-party Stallari blade MCP. Reference implementation for virtualmachine-v1 contract. TypeScript, dual transport (stdio + HTTP). GPU-first defaults. Dual write-gate pattern.
